name: core_mem_ignores_other_sbufs
code:
proof fn phi_3_core_mem_ignores_other_sbufs(s: State, c1: Core, c2: Core)
    requires
        c1 !== c2,
        s.sbuf.contains_key(c1),
        s.sbuf.contains_key(c2),
        s.sbuf[c2].len() > 0,
    ensures
        s.core_mem(c1) == (State { sbuf: s.sbuf.insert(c2, Seq::empty()), ..s }).core_mem(c1),
{
}
reason: `core_mem` only applies the store buffer of the given core — modifications to other cores' store buffers are invisible, meaning each core sees a potentially stale view of page table memory

name: core_mem_pml4_relies_on_external_body
reason: The proof that `core_mem` preserves `pml4` depends on `lemma_write_seq` (external_body) — the entire per-core memory consistency guarantee rests on an unverified lemma

name: writes_tso_not_connected_to_sbuf
reason: Adding to a core's store buffer doesn't update `writes.tso` — the TSO tracking set and the actual store buffers are disconnected spec-level fields with no invariant linking them
